vocabulary.example: While the Gryffindor common room and the Great Hall had roaring fires, the drafty corridors had become icy and a bitter wind rattled the windows in the classrooms.

vocabulary.definition: a decorative chain of flowers, leaves, or other material hung in a loop
vocabulary.example: Festoons of holly and mistletoe hung all around the walls, and no less than twelve towering Christmas trees stood around the room, some sparkling with tiny icicles, some glittering with hundreds of candles.

vocabulary.definition: a way of speaking slowly, stretching out the words
vocabulary.example: “Would you mind moving out of the way?” came Malfoy’s cold drawl from behind them.
“Just have to find out for ourselves, then,” said Ron, and they left Hagrid looking disgruntled and hurried off to the library.

vocabulary.definition: to wave or hold something in a threatening or excited way
vocabulary.example: Madam Pince the librarian brandished a feather duster at him.

vocabulary.definition: to carve wood by cutting small pieces off
vocabulary.example: Inside was a roughly cut wooden flute. Hagrid had obviously whittled it himself.

vocabulary.definition: to slide smoothly, like a snake
vocabulary.example: Something fluid and silvery gray went slithering to the floor where it lay in gleaming folds.

vocabulary.definition: the state of not being seen
vocabulary.example: “It’s an Invisibility Cloak,” said Ron, a look of awe on his face.
It was wrapped in thick brown paper and scrawled across it was To Harry, from Hagrid.

vocabulary.definition: so frightening it seems to freeze your blood
vocabulary.example: A piercing, bloodcurdling shriek split the silence—the book was screaming!

vocabulary.definition: so fascinated that you cannot look away
vocabulary.example: Men have wasted away before it, entranced by what they have seen, or been driven mad, not knowing if what it shows is real or even possible.

vocabulary.definition: unable to see things clearly unless they are close
vocabulary.example: “Strange how nearsighted being invisible can make you,” said Dumbledore, and Harry was relieved to see that he was smiling.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Stays at Hogwarts for Christmas, receives an Invisibility Cloak, and discovers the Mirror of Erised.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Shares Christmas with Harry and sees himself as Head Boy in the mirror.

characters.name: Albus Dumbledore
characters.description: Finds Harry at the mirror and explains what the Mirror of Erised shows.

characters.name: Draco Malfoy
characters.description: Taunts Harry about having nowhere to go for the holidays.

background.hook: Snow covers Hogwarts as Christmas arrives. Harry gets unexpected presents — including a rare Invisibility Cloak — and at night he finds a magical mirror that shows him the family he has never known.
background.readingFocus: Watch what gifts Harry receives, what happens when he uses the cloak in the library, and what different people see in the Mirror of Erised.

miniCheck.stem: An Invisibility Cloak makes the wearer —
If you are entranced by something, you are —

options.text: carve wood by cutting small pieces away

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry's first Christmas at Hogwarts, his Invisibility Cloak, and the Mirror of Erised
Harry's first Quidditch match against Slytherin
Harry and Ron fighting a mountain troll
Hagrid raising a Norwegian Ridgeback dragon

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers Christmas at Hogwarts, Harry's presents, nights with the mirror, and Dumbledore's warning about dwelling on dreams.

comprehension.stem: What does Hagrid give Harry for Christmas?
comprehension.explanation: Hagrid's parcel contains a wooden flute he whittled himself.

comprehension.stem: What is inside Harry's last Christmas parcel?

options.text: An Invisibility Cloak and a note saying his father left it
A signed copy of Quidditch Through the Ages
A map of the Forbidden Forest

comprehension.explanation: Silvery cloth slithers to the floor, and Ron identifies it as an Invisibility Cloak with a note from Harry's father.

comprehension.stem: What does Harry see in the Mirror of Erised?

options.text: His parents and other family members smiling at him
Himself holding the Quidditch cup as captain

comprehension.explanation: Harry sees his mother, father, and other relatives with his green eyes and knobbly knees.

comprehension.stem: What does Ron see when he stands in front of the mirror?

options.text: Himself as Head Boy holding the house and Quidditch cups

comprehension.explanation: Ron sees himself older, wearing the Head Boy badge, and holding the house and Quidditch cups.

comprehension.stem: Why does Harry go to the Restricted Section on Christmas night?

options.text: He wants to find out who Nicolas Flamel is
Madam Pince invites him for extra credit
Snape sets him a detention there
He is looking for another mirror

comprehension.explanation: Under the cloak, Harry hopes to read until he learns who Flamel is.

comprehension.stem: What does Dumbledore tell Harry about the mirror?

options.text: It shows people's deepest desires and will be moved; Harry must not go looking for it again
It predicts the future and should be used every night
It belongs to Snape and must be returned at once
It only works on Christmas Eve

comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore explains the mirror shows the deepest desire of one's heart and warns that it does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.

vocabulary.stem: If a place is drafty, it —

options.text: is locked with a special charm
is hidden under an Invisibility Cloak

vocabulary.explanation: Drafty corridors grow icy while the common room stays warm.

vocabulary.stem: Something fluid and silvery gray went ___ to the floor where it lay in gleaming folds.
vocabulary.explanation: The silvery Invisibility Cloak slithers to the floor when Harry unwraps it.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'disgruntled'?
vocabulary.explanation: Hagrid looks disgruntled after they keep asking about Nicolas Flamel.

vocabulary.stem: A piercing, ___ shriek split the silence—the book was screaming!
vocabulary.explanation: A screaming book in the Restricted Section lets out a bloodcurdling shriek.

options.text: wave it in a threatening or showy way
wrap it as a Christmas gift

vocabulary.explanation: Madam Pince brandishes a feather duster at Harry in the Restricted Section.

cloze.title: The Mirror of Erised
paragraphs: Snow covers Hogwarts as Christmas nears. (1) ___ corridors grow icy, and the Great Hall is hung with (2) ___ of holly and mistletoe around twelve towering trees.
Harry stays for the holidays and opens presents, including a flute Hagrid (3) ___ and a silvery (4) ___ Cloak that once belonged to his father.

Wearing the cloak, Harry sneaks into the library's Restricted Section, where a book lets out a (5) ___ shriek. Fleeing Filch and Snape, he hides in a room with the Mirror of (7) ___ and sees his family.
Ron later sees himself as Head Boy, but Dumbledore warns that people can become (6) ___ by the mirror and says it shows the deepest desire of the heart.

gaps.explanation: Drafty corridors turn icy while fires burn in the common room.
Festoons of holly and mistletoe decorate the Great Hall.
Hagrid whittled the wooden flute himself.
Ron identifies the silvery cloak as an Invisibility Cloak.
A Restricted Section book screams with a bloodcurdling shriek.
Dumbledore says men have wasted away before the mirror, entranced by what they see.
Dumbledore says Harry has discovered the delights of the Mirror of Erised.
